config=config,\n            run_step=failing_runner,\n        )\n\n        # First step (extract) should fail gate (no output written)\n        assert results[-1].status == StepStatus.FAIL\n        assert len(results) < 9  # Not all steps executed\n\n\nclass TestContextIsolation:\n    def test_subprocess_uses_mock(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"Verify all tests use unittest.mock.patch for subprocess isolation.\"\"\"\n        # This is a meta-test documenting that integration tests\n        # use mock runners rather than real subprocess calls.\n        with patch(\"subprocess.Popen\") as mock_popen:\n            # If any test accidentally called subprocess.Popen directly,\n            # this would be caught. Our mock runner pattern avoids that.\n            assert not mock_popen.called\n\n\nclass TestSaveAndReloadState:\n    def test_state_roundtrip(self, tmp_path):\n        config = _make_config(tmp_path)\n        step = Step(\n            id=\"extract\", prompt=\"p\", output_file=config.output_dir / \"extract.md\",\n            gate=None, timeout_seconds=300,\n        )\n        results = [\n            StepResult(\n                step=step,\n                status=StepStatus.PASS,\n                attempt=1,\n                started_at=_now(),\n                finished_at=_now(),\n            ),\n        ]\n\n        _save_state(config, results)\n\n        from superclaude.cli.roadmap.executor import read_state\n        state = read_state(config.output_dir / \".roadmap-state.json\")\n        assert state is not None\n        assert state[\"schema_version\"] == 1\n        assert state[\"steps\"][\"extract\"][\"status\"] == \"PASS\"\n\n\nclass TestSanitizeOutput:\n    \"\"\"Unit tests for _sanitize_output() -- spec §6.2 test matrix.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_strips_preamble_before_frontmatter(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"Preamble present: file is rewritten, returns byte count.\"\"\"\n        f = tmp_path / \"output.md\"\n        preamble = \"Here is the result:\\n\\n\"\n        body = \"---\\ntitle: test\\n---\\n## Content\\n- item 1\\n\"\n        f.write_text(preamble + body, encoding=\"utf-8\")\n\n        result = _sanitize_output(f)\n\n        assert result == len(preamble.encode(\"utf-8\"))\n        assert f.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\") == body\n\n    def test_no_preamble_unchanged(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"No preamble (starts with ---): file unchanged, returns 0.\"\"\"\n        f = tmp_path / \"output.md\"\n        content = \"---\\ntitle: test\\n---\\n## Content\\n\"\n        f.write_text(content, encoding=\"utf-8\")\n\n        result = _sanitize_output(f)\n\n        assert result == 0\n        assert f.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\") == content\n\n    def test_no_frontmatter_unchanged(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"No frontmatter delimiter at all: file unchanged, returns 0.\"\"\"\n        f = tmp_path / \"output.md\"\n        content = \"Just some text without any frontmatter.\\n\"\n        f.write_text(content, encoding=\"utf-8\")\n\n        result = _sanitize_output(f)\n\n        assert result == 0\n        assert f.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\") == content\n\n    def test_multi_line_preamble_stripped(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"Multi-line preamble: all lines before first --- are stripped.\"\"\"\n        f = tmp_path / \"output.md\"\n        preamble = \"Sure! Here is your output.\\n\\nI've formatted it as requested:\\n\\n\"\n        body = \"---\\nkey: value\\n---\\n- item\\n\"\n        f.write_text(preamble + body, encoding=\"utf-8\")\n\n        result = _sanitize_output(f)\n\n        assert result == len(preamble.encode(\"utf-8\"))\n        assert f.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\") == body\n\n    def test_atomic_write_uses_tmp_and_replace(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"Atomic write: .tmp file used and os.replace() called.\"\"\"\n        f = tmp_path / \"output.md\"\n        preamble = \"preamble\\n\"\n        body = \"---\\ntitle: test\\n---\\n\"\n        f.write_text(preamble + body, encoding=\"utf-8\")\n\n        with patch(\"os.replace\", wraps=__import__(\"os\").replace) as mock_replace:\n            result = _sanitize_output(f)\n\n        assert result > 0\n        # os.replace was called with the .tmp source and original target\n        mock_replace.assert_called_once()\n        call_args = mock_replace.call_args[0]\n        assert str(call_args[0]).endswith(\".tmp\")\n        assert call_args[1] == f\n        # Final file has correct content\n        assert f.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\") == body\n","numLines":284,"startLine":1,"totalLines":284}}}

AgentSpec(\"opus\", \"architect\")\n            AgentSpec.parse(\"haiku\")          -> AgentSpec(\"haiku\", \"architect\")\n        \"\"\"\n        if \":\" in spec:\n            model, persona = spec.split(\":\", 1)\n            return cls(model=model.strip(), persona=persona.strip())\n        return cls(model=spec.strip(), persona=\"architect\")\n\n    @property\n    def id(self) -> str:\n It only skips the post-pipeline validation\n    subsystem.\n\n    Args:\n        auto_accept: When True, the spec-patch resume cycle skips the\n            interactive prompt and proceeds automatically if evidence is\n            found. When False (default), the cycle prompts the user.\n            This is an internal parameter, not exposed on the CLI.\n    \"\"\"\n    config.output_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n\n    # FR-2.24.1.11: Recursion guard — local variable, per-invocation\n    _spec_patch_cycle_count = 0\n\n    # FR-2.24.1.9 Condition 3: Capture spec hash at function entry\n    initial_spec_hash = hashlib.sha256(config.spec_file.read_bytes()).hexdigest()\n\n    steps = _build_steps(config)\n\n    # --dry-run: print plan and exit\n    if config.dry_run:\n        _dry_run_output(steps)\n        return\n\n    # --resume: check which steps already pass their gates\n    if resume:\n        from ..pipeline.gates import gate_passed\n\n        steps = _apply_resume(steps, config, gate_passed)\n\n    # Execute pipeline\n    results = execute_pipeline(\n        steps=steps,\n        config=config,\n        run_step=roadmap_run_step,\n        on_step_start=_print_step_start,\n        on_step_complete=_print_step_complete,\n    )\n\n    # Save state\n    _save_state(config, results)\n\n    # Check for failures\n    failures = [r for r in results if r.status in (StepStatus.FAIL, StepStatus.TIMEOUT)]\n    if failures:\n        # FR-2.24.1.9: Check if spec-fidelity failed and auto-resume is possible\n        spec_fidelity_failed = any(\n            r.step and r.step.id == \"spec-fidelity\"\n            and r.status in (StepStatus.FAIL, StepStatus.TIMEOUT)\n            for r in results\n        )\n\n        if spec_fidelity_failed:\n            resumed = _apply_resume_after_spec_patch(\n                config=config,\n                results=results,\n                auto_accept=auto_accept,\n                initial_spec_hash=initial_spec_hash,\n                cycle_count=_spec_patch_cycle_count,\n            )\n            if resumed:\n                _spec_patch_cycle_count += 1\n                # Cycle complete — resumed pipeline ran to completion or failure\n                # inside _apply_resume_after_spec_patch. If it returned True,\n                # we're done (either success or failure was handled internally).\n                return\n\n        halt_msg = _format_halt_output(results, config)\n        print(halt_msg, file=sys.stderr)\n        sys.exit(1)\n\n    print(f\"\\n[roadmap] Pipeline complete: {len(results)} steps passed\", flush=True)\n\n    # Auto-invoke validation after successful pipeline completion\n    if no_validate:\n        print(\"[roadmap] Validation skipped (--no-validate)\", flush=True)\n        _save_validation_status(config, \"skipped\")\n        return\n\n    # Check if validation already completed (--resume path)\n    if resume:\n        state_file = config.output_dir / \".roadmap-state.json\"\n        state = read_state(state_file)\n        if state and \"validation\" in state:\n            saved = state[\"validation\"]\n            if saved.get(\"status\") in (\"pass\", \"fail\"):\n                print(\n                    f\"[roadmap] Validation already completed ({saved['status']}), skipping\",\n                    flush=True,\n                )\n                return\n\n    _auto_invoke_validate(config)\n\n\n\ndef _find_qualifying_deviation_files(\n    config: RoadmapConfig,\n    results: list[StepResult],\n) -> list:\n    \"\"\"Find deviation files written after spec-fidelity started.\n\n    Returns qualifying DeviationRecord objects, or empty list if\n    conditions are not met.\n","numLines":120,"startLine":830,"totalLines":1358}}}

Unresolved references emit warnings but\n    do not block the pipeline (warning-only mode per OQ-001 for v2.20).\n    \"\"\"\n    import re\n    import warnings\n\n    # Extract heading anchors (simplified: heading text, lowercased)\n    headings: set[str] = set()\n    for line in content.splitlines():\n        stripped = line.lstrip()\n        if stripped.startswith(\"#\"):\n            heading_text = stripped.lstrip(\"#\").strip().lower()\n            headings.add(heading_text)\n\n    # Check each reference against headings\n    unresolved: list[str] = []\n    for ref in refs:\n        found = any(ref in h for h in headings)\n        if not found:\n            # Also check if the section number appears as a heading prefix\n            found = any(h.startswith(ref) for h in headings)\n        if not found:\n            unresolved.append(ref)\n\n    if unresolved:\n        for ref in unresolved:\n            warnings.warn(\n                f\"Unresolved cross-reference: 'See section {ref}' has no matching heading\",\n                stacklevel=2,\n            )\n        # Unresolved references emit warnings but\n    do not block the pipeline (warning-only mode per OQ-001 for v2.20).\n    \"\"\"\n    import re\n    import warnings\n\n    # Extract heading anchors (simplified: heading text, lowercased)\n    headings: set[str] = set()\n    for line in content.splitlines():\n        stripped = line.lstrip()\n        if stripped.startswith(\"#\"):\n            heading_text = stripped.lstrip(\"#\").strip().lower()\n            headings.add(heading_text)\n\n    # Unresolved references emit warnings but\n    do not block the pipeline (warning-only mode per OQ-001 for v2.20).\n    \"\"\"\n    import re\n    import warnings\n\n    # Extract heading anchors (simplified: heading text, lowercased)\n    headings: set[str] = set()\n    for line in content.splitlines():\n        stripped = line.lstrip()\n        if stripped.startswith(\"#\"):\n            heading_text = stripped.lstrip(\"#\").strip().lower()\n            headings.add(heading_text)\n\n    # Check each reference against headings\n    unresolved: list[str] = []\n    for ref in refs:\n        found = any(ref in h for h in headings)\n        if not found:\n            # Also check if the section number appears as a heading prefix\n            found = any(h.startswith(ref) for h in headings)\n        if not found:\n            unresolved.append(ref)\n\n    if unresolved:\n        for ref in unresolved:\n            warnings.warn(\n                f\"Unresolved cross-reference: 'See section {ref}' has no matching heading\",\n                stacklevel=2,\n            )\n        # NFR-004, except where explicitly noted.\n\nProvides:\n- format_validation_summary(findings) -> str  (T03.01)\n- RemediationScope enum  (T03.01/T03.02)\n- filter_findings(findings, scope) -> tuple  (T03.02)\n- generate_remediation_tasklist(findings, source_report_path, source_report_content) -> str  (T03.04)\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport enum\nimport hashlib\nfrom datetime import datetime, timezone\n\nfrom .models import Finding\n\n\nclass RemediationScope(enum.Enum):\n    \"\"\"User-selected scope for remediation filtering.\n\n    Maps to prompt options:\n    [1] BLOCKING_ONLY  -- only BLOCKING findings\n    [2] BLOCKING_WARNING -- BLOCKING + WARNING\n    [3] ALL -- all findings with fix_guidance\n    \"\"\"\n\n    BLOCKING_ONLY = \"blocking_only\"\n    BLOCKING_WARNING = \"blocking_warning\"\n    ALL = \"all\"\n\n\n# Agreement categories that trigger auto-SKIP regardless of scope selection\n_AUTO_SKIP_CATEGORIES = frozenset({\"NO_ACTION_REQUIRED\", \"OUT_OF_SCOPE\"})\n\n\ndef format_validation_summary(findings: list[Finding]) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Format findings into a severity-grouped terminal summary.\n\n    Groups findings by severity (BLOCKING, WARNING, INFO) with counts\n    and lists each finding's ID and description. Returns a formatted\n    string suitable for terminal output per spec section 2.2 box layout.\n\n    Pure function: no I/O or side effects.\n    \"\"\"\n    blocking = [f for f in findings if f.severity == \"BLOCKING\"]\n    warning = [f for f in findings if f.severity == \"WARNING\"]\n    info = [f for f in findings if f.severity == \"INFO\"]\n\n    lines: list[str] = []\n    lines.append(\"=\" * 60)\n    lines.append(\"  VALIDATION SUMMARY\")\n    lines.append(\"=\" * 60)\n    lines.append(\"\")\n\n    total = len(findings)\n    lines.append(f\"  Total findings: {total}\")\n    lines.append(f\"  BLOCKING: {len(blocking)}  |  WARNING: {len(warning)}  |  INFO: {len(info)}\")\n    lines.append(\"\")\n\n    if blocking:\n        lines.append(\"  --- BLOCKING ---\")\n        for f in blocking:\n            lines.append(f\"  {f.id} | {f.description}\")\n        lines.append(\"\")\n\n    if warning:\n        lines.append(\"  --- WARNING ---\")\n        for f in warning:\n            lines.append(f\"  {f.id} | {f.description}\")\n        lines.append(\"\")\n\n    if info:\n        lines.append(\"  --- INFO ---\")\n        for f in info:\n            lines.append(f\"  {f.id} | {f.description}\")\n        lines.append(\"\")\n\n    lines.append(\"=\" * 60)\n    return \"\\n\".join(lines)\n\n\ndef should_skip_prompt(findings: list[Finding]) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Return True when prompt should be skipped (zero BLOCKING + zero WARNING).\n\n    Per spec section 2.2: when there are no BLOCKING and no WARNING findings,\n    the prompt is auto-skipped.\n\n    Pure function: no I/O or side effects.\n    \"\"\"\n    blocking = sum(1 for f in findings if f.severity == \"BLOCKING\")\n    warning = sum(1 for f in findings if f.severity == \"WARNING\")\n    return blocking == 0 and warning == 0\n\n\ndef filter_findings(\n    findings: list[Finding],\n    scope: RemediationScope,\n) -> tuple[list[Finding], list[Finding]]:\n    \"\"\"Filter findings by user-selected scope with auto-SKIP logic.\n\n    Auto-SKIP is applied first: findings with agreement_category of\n    NO_ACTION_REQUIRED or OUT_OF_SCOPE are always moved to skipped,\n    regardless of scope selection.\n\n    Then scope filtering is applied:\n    - BLOCKING_ONLY: keeps only BLOCKING severity\n    - BLOCKING_WARNING: keeps BLOCKING + WARNING\n    - ALL: keeps all findings that have fix_guidance\n\n    Returns (actionable, skipped) tuple.\n\n    Pure function: no I/O or side effects.\n    \"\"\"\n    actionable: list[Finding] = []\n    skipped: list[Finding] = []\n\n    for f in findings:\n        # Auto-SKIP: NO_ACTION_REQUIRED and OUT_OF_SCOPE always skipped\n        if f.agreement_category in _AUTO_SKIP_CATEGORIES:\n            skipped.append(f)\n            continue\n\n        # Already terminal status (FIXED, SKIPPED) -> skip\n        if f.status in (\"FIXED\", \"SKIPPED\"):\n            skipped.append(f)\n            continue\n\n        # Scope filtering\n        if scope == RemediationScope.BLOCKING_ONLY:\n            if f.severity == \"BLOCKING\":\n                actionable.append(f)\n            else:\n                skipped.append(f)\n        elif scope == RemediationScope.BLOCKING_WARNING:\n            if f.severity in (\"BLOCKING\", \"WARNING\"):\n                actionable.append(f)\n            else:\n                skipped.append(f)\n        else:  # ALL\n            if f.fix_guidance:\n                actionable.append(f)\n            else:\n                skipped.append(f)\n\n    return actionable, skipped\n\n\ndef generate_remediation_tasklist(\n    findings: list[Finding],\n    source_report_path: str,\n    source_report_content: str,\n) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Generate remediation-tasklist.md with YAML frontmatter.\n\n    Produces markdown with:\n    - YAML frontmatter: type, source_report, source_report_hash, generated,\n      total_findings, actionable, skipped\n    - Severity-grouped entries: - [ ] F-XX | file | STATUS -- description\n\n    The source_report_hash is SHA-256 of the source report content.\n\n    Pure function: no I/O or side effects (datetime injected via UTC now).\n    \"\"\"\n    source_hash = hashlib.sha256(source_report_content.encode(\"utf-8\")).hexdigest()\n    generated = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()\n\n    actionable_findings = [f for f in findings if f.status == \"PENDING\"]\n    skipped_findings = [f for f in findings if f.status != \"PENDING\"]\n    total = len(findings)\n\n    # YAML frontmatter\n    lines: list[str] = [\n        \"---\",\n        \"type: remediation-tasklist\",\n        f\"source_report: {source_report_path}\",\n        f\"source_report_hash: {source_hash}\",\n        f\"generated: {generated}\",\n        f\"total_findings: {total}\",\n        f\"actionable: {len(actionable_findings)}\",\n        f\"skipped: {len(skipped_findings)}\",\n        \"---\",\n        \"\",\n        \"# Remediation Tasklist\",\n        \"\",\n    ]\n\n    # Group by severity\n    blocking = [f for f in findings if f.severity == \"BLOCKING\"]\n    warning = [f for f in findings if f.severity == \"WARNING\"]\n    info = [f for f in findings if f.severity == \"INFO\"]\n    skipped_sev = [f for f in findings if f.status == \"SKIPPED\"]\n\n    if blocking:\n        lines.append(\"## BLOCKING\")\n        lines.append(\"\")\n        for f in blocking:\n            status = f.status\n            files = \", \".join(f.files_affected) if f.files_affected else \"unknown\"\n            lines.append(f\"- [ ] {f.id} | {files} | {status} -- {f.description}\")\n        lines.append(\"\")\n\n    if warning:\n        lines.append(\"## WARNING\")\n        lines.append(\"\")\n        for f in warning:\n            status = f.status\n            files = \", \".join(f.files_affected) if f.files_affected else \"unknown\"\n            lines.append(f\"- [ ] {f.id} | {files} | {status} -- {f.description}\")\n        lines.append(\"\")\n\n    if info:\n        lines.append(\"## INFO\")\n        lines.append(\"\")\n        for f in info:\n            status = f.status\n            files = \", \".join(f.files_affected) if f.files_affected else \"unknown\"\n            lines.append(f\"- [ ] {f.id} | {files} | {status} -- {f.description}\")\n        lines.append(\"\")\n\n    # Skipped section (findings that were already SKIPPED before tasklist generation)\n    if skipped_sev:\n        lines.append(\"## SKIPPED\")\n        lines.append(\"\")\n        for f in skipped_sev:\n            files = \", \".join(f.files_affected) if f.files_affected else \"unknown\"\n            lines.append(f\"- [x] {f.id} | {files} | SKIPPED -- {f.description}\")\n        lines.append(\"\")\n\n    return \"\\n\".join(lines)\n\n\ndef generate_stub_tasklist(\n    source_report_path: str,\n    source_report_content: str,\n) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Generate a stub Fail-closed for all error conditions.\n\n    On hash mismatch, resets spec-fidelity and deviation-analysis gate\n    pass state to force re-run (FR-084).\n\n    Returns:\n        True if hash matches (fresh), False for any failure or mismatch.\n    \"\"\"\n    spec_deviations = output_dir / \"spec-deviations.md\"\n\n    # Case 1: spec-deviations.md not found\n    if not spec_deviations.exists():\n        _log.warning(\"Freshness check: spec-deviations.md not found\")\n        return False\n\n    # Case 2: Read error\n    try:\n        content = spec_deviations.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n    except OSError as e:\n        _log.warning(\"Freshness check: read error for spec-deviations.md: %s\", e)\n        return False\n\n    # Case 3: Empty file\n    if not content.strip():\n content.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport hashlib\nimport json\nimport logging\nimport sys\nimport time\nfrom datetime import datetime, timezone\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom 128 * 1024\n# 2.3x safety factor; measured template peak ~3.4 KB\n_PROMPT_TEMPLATE_OVERHEAD = 8 * 1024\n# Derivation: MAX_ARG_STRLEN - PROMPT_TEMPLATE_OVERHEAD = 120 KB = 122,880 bytes\n_EMBED_SIZE_LIMIT = _MAX_ARG_STRLEN - _PROMPT_TEMPLATE_OVERHEAD\nassert _PROMPT_TEMPLATE_OVERHEAD >= 4096, (\n    \"Kernel margin violated: _PROMPT_TEMPLATE_OVERHEAD must be >=4096 bytes \"\n    \"to stay safely below MAX_ARG_STRLEN=128 KB; measured template peak ~3.4 KB\"\n)\n\n\ndef _embed_inputs(input_paths: list[Path]) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Read input files and return their contents as fenced code blocks.\n\n    Each file is wrapped in a fenced block with a ``# <path>`` header.\n    Returns an empty string when *input_paths* is empty (no-op).\n    \"\"\"\n    if not input_paths:\n        return \"\"\n\n    blocks: list[str] = []\n    for p in input_paths:\n        content = Path(p).read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n        blocks.append(f\"# {p}\\n```\\n{content}\\n```\")\n the first ``^---`` line, and removes everything before\n    it.  Uses atomic write (write to ``.tmp`` then ``os.replace()``) to prevent\n    partial file states.\n\n    Returns the byte count of the stripped preamble (0 when file already starts\n    with ``---`` or no frontmatter delimiter is found).\n    \"\"\"\n    import os\n    import re\n\n    try:\n        content = output_file.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n    except FileNotFoundError:\n        return 0\n\n    # Already starts with frontmatter delimiter -- nothing to strip\n    if content.lstrip().startswith(\"---\"):\n        return 0\n\n    # Search for the first ^--- line\n    match = re.search(r\"^---[ \\t]*$\", content, re.MULTILINE)\n    if match is None:\n        # No frontmatter found at all -- leave file unchanged\n        return 0\n\n    preamble = content[: match.start()]\n    cleaned = content[match.start() :]\n    preamble_bytes = len(preamble.encode(\"utf-8\"))\n\n    # Atomic write: tmp file + os.replace\n    tmp_file = output_file.with_suffix(output_file.suffix + \".tmp\")\n    tmp_file.write_text(cleaned, encoding=\"utf-8\")\n    os.replace(tmp_file, output_file)\n\n    _log.info(\"Stripped %d-byte preamble from %s\", preamble_bytes, output_file)\n    return preamble_bytes\n\n\ndef _inject_pipeline_diagnostics(\n    output_file: Path,\n    started_at: datetime,\n    finished_at: datetime,\n) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Inject executor-populated pipeline_diagnostics into extraction frontmatter.\n\n    The LLM cannot reliably produce execution timing or environment metadata,\n    so the executor injects these fields post-subprocess (FR-033).\n    \"\"\"\n    content = output_file.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n    if not content.startswith(\"---\"):\n        return\n\n    # Find end of frontmatter\n    end_idx = content.find(\"\\n---\", 3)\n    if end_idx == -1:\n        return\n\n    elapsed = (finished_at - started_at).total_seconds()\n    diagnostics_line = (\n        f\"pipeline_diagnostics: \"\n        f\"{{elapsed_seconds: {elapsed:.1f}, \"\n        f\"started_at: \\\"{started_at.isoformat()}\\\", \"\n        f\"finished_at: \\\"{finished_at.isoformat()}\\\"}}\"\n    )\n\n    # Insert before the closing ---\n    new_content = (\n        content[: end_idx]\n        + \"\\n\"\n        + diagnostics_line\n        + content[end_idx:]\n    )\n    output_file.write_text(new_content, encoding=\"utf-8\")\n\n\ndef roadmap_run_step(\n    step: Step,\n    config: PipelineConfig,\n    cancel_check: Callable[[], bool],\n) -> StepResult:\n    \"\"\"Execute a single roadmap step as a Claude subprocess.\n\n    Builds argv with context isolation, launches process, waits with\n    timeout, and returns StepResult.\n    \"\"\"\n    started_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n\n    if config.dry_run:\n        _log.info(\"[dry-run] Would execute step '%s'\", step.id)\n        return StepResult(\n            step=step,\n            status=StepStatus.PASS,\n            attempt=1,\n            started_at=started_at,\n            finished_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc),\n        )\n\n    # Inline embedding: read input files into the prompt instead of --file flags\n    # --file is broken (cloud download mechanism, not local file injector) so\n    # inline embedding is always used regardless of composed prompt size.\n    embedded = _embed_inputs(step.inputs)\n    if embedded:\n        composed = step.prompt + \"\\n\\n\" + embedded\n        if len(composed.encode(\"utf-8\")) > _EMBED_SIZE_LIMIT:\n            # <= is intentional; _EMBED_SIZE_LIMIT = 120 KB is safely below MAX_ARG_STRLEN = 128 KB\n            _log.warning(\n                \"Step '%s': composed prompt exceeds %d bytes; embedding inline anyway\"\n                \" (--file fallback is unavailable)\",\n                step.id,\n                _EMBED_SIZE_LIMIT,\n            )\n        effective_prompt = composed\n        extra_args: list[str] = []\n    else:\n        effective_prompt = step.prompt\n        extra_args = []\n\n    proc = ClaudeProcess(\n        prompt=effective_prompt,\n        output_file=step.output_file,\n        error_file=step.output_file.with_suffix(\".err\"),\n        max_turns=config.max_turns,\n        model=step.model or config.model,\n        permission_flag=config.permission_flag,\n        timeout_seconds=step.timeout_seconds,\n        output_format=\"text\",\n        extra_args=extra_args,\n    )\n\n    proc.start()\n\n    # Poll for cancellation while waiting\n    while proc._process is not None and proc._process.poll() is None:\n        if cancel_check():\n            proc.terminate()\n            return StepResult(\n                step=step,\n                status=StepStatus.CANCELLED,\n                attempt=1,\n                gate_failure_reason=\"Cancelled by external signal\",\n                started_at=started_at,\n                finished_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc),\n            )\n        time.sleep(1)\n\n    exit_code = proc.wait()\n    finished_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n\n    if exit_code == 124:\n        return StepResult(\n            step=step,\n            status=StepStatus.TIMEOUT,\n            attempt=1,\n            gate_failure_reason=f\"Step '{step.id}' timed out after {step.timeout_seconds}s\",\n            started_at=started_at,\n            finished_at=finished_at,\n        )\n\n    if exit_code != 0:\n        return StepResult(\n            step=step,\n            status=StepStatus.FAIL,\n            attempt=1,\n            gate_failure_reason=f\"Step '{step.id}' exited with code {exit_code}\",\n            started_at=started_at,\n            finished_at=finished_at,\n        )\n\n    # Sanitize output: strip conversational preamble before gate validation\n    _sanitize_output(step.output_file)\n\n    # Inject executor-populated fields into extract step frontmatter (FR-033)\n    if step.id == \"extract\" and step.output_file.exists():\n        _inject_pipeline_diagnostics(step.output_file, started_at, finished_at)\n\n    # Process completed successfully; gate check happens in execute_pipeline\n    return StepResult(\n        step=step,\n        status=StepStatus.PASS,\n        attempt=1,\n        started_at=started_at,\n        finished_at=finished_at,\n    )\n\n\ndef build_certify_step(\n    config: RoadmapConfig,\n    findings: list | None = None,\n    context_sections: dict[str, str] | None = None,\n) -> Step:\n    \"\"\"Build a certify Step for execution via execute_pipeline().\n\n    The certify step runs as a standard Step (not ClaudeProcess directly)\n    per spec section 2.5. inputs=[extraction],\n                retry_limit=1,\n                model=agent_a.model,\n            ),\n            Step(\n                id=f\"generate-{agent_b.id}\",\n prompt=build_diff_prompt(roadmap_a, roadmap_b),\n            output_file=diff_file,\n            gate=DIFF_GATE,\n            timeout_seconds=300,\n            inputs=[roadmap_a, roadmap_b],\n            retry_limit=1,\n        ),\n        # Step 4: Debate\n        Step(\n            id=\"debate\",\n            prompt=build_debate_prompt(diff_file, roadmap_a, roadmap_b, config.depth),\n            output_file=debate_file,\n            gate=DEBATE_GATE,\n            timeout_seconds=600,\n            inputs=[diff_file, roadmap_a, roadmap_b],\n            retry_limit=1,\n        ),\n        # Step 5: Score\n        Step(\n            id=\"score\",\n            prompt=build_score_prompt(debate_file, roadmap_a, roadmap_b),\n            output_file=score_file,\n            gate=SCORE_GATE,\n            timeout_seconds=300,\n            inputs=[debate_file, roadmap_a, roadmap_b],\n            retry_limit=1,\n        ),\n        # Step 6: Merge\n        Step(\n            id=\"merge\",\n            prompt=build_merge_prompt(score_file, roadmap_a, roadmap_b, debate_file),\n            output_file=merge_file,\n            gate=MERGE_GATE,\n            timeout_seconds=600,\n            inputs=[score_file, roadmap_a, roadmap_b, debate_file],\n            retry_limit=1,\n        ),\n        # Step 7: Test Strategy\n        Step(\n            id=\"test-strategy\",\n            prompt=build_test_strategy_prompt(merge_file, extraction),\n            output_file=test_strat,\n            gate=TEST_STRATEGY_GATE,\n            timeout_seconds=300,\n            inputs=[merge_file, extraction],\n            retry_limit=1,\n        ),\n        # Step 8: Spec Fidelity (after test-strategy, FR-008 through FR-010)\n        Step(\n            id=\"spec-fidelity\",\n            prompt=build_spec_fidelity_prompt(config.spec_file, merge_file),\n            output_file=spec_fidelity_file,\n            gate=SPEC_FIDELITY_GATE,\n            timeout_seconds=600,\n            inputs=[config.spec_file, merge_file],\n            retry_limit=1,\n        ),\n    ]\n\n    return steps\n\n\ndef _format_halt_output(results: list[StepResult], config: RoadmapConfig) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Format HALT diagnostic output per spec section 6.2.\"\"\"\n    failed = [r for r in results if r.status in (StepStatus.FAIL, StepStatus.TIMEOUT)]\n    passed = [r for r in results if r.status == StepStatus.PASS]\n    cancelled = [r for r in results if r.status == StepStatus.CANCELLED]\n\n    if not failed:\n        return \"\"\n\n    fail = failed[-1]\n    step = fail.step\n\n    # Calculate file details if output exists\n    file_info = \"\"\n    if step and step.output_file.exists():\n        content = step.output_file.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n        byte_count = len(content.encode(\"utf-8\"))\n        line_count = len(content.splitlines())\n        file_info = f\"  Output size: {byte_count} bytes ({line_count} lines)\\n\"\n    elif step:\n        file_info = \"  Output file: not created\\n\"\n\n    elapsed = f\"{fail.duration_seconds:.0f}s\"\n\n    lines = [\n        f\"ERROR: Roadmap pipeline halted at step '{step.id}' (attempt {fail.attempt}/2)\",\n        f\"  Gate failure: {fail.gate_failure_reason}\",\n        f\"  Output file: {step.output_file}\",\n        file_info.rstrip(),\n        f\"  Step timeout: {step.timeout_seconds}s | Elapsed: {elapsed}\",\n        \"\",\n        f\"Completed steps: {', '.join(f'{r.step.id} (PASS, attempt {r.attempt})' for r in passed) or 'none'}\",\n        f\"Failed step:     {step.id} ({fail.status.value}, attempt {fail.attempt})\",\n    ]\n\n    # Collect skipped steps\n    all_step_ids = _get_all_step_ids(config)\n    executed_ids = {r.step.id for r in results}\n    skipped_ids = [sid for sid in all_step_ids if sid not in executed_ids]\n    if cancelled:\n        skipped_ids = [r.step.id for r in cancelled] + skipped_ids\n\n    if skipped_ids:\n        lines.append(f\"Skipped steps:   {', '.join(skipped_ids)}\")\n\n    lines.extend([\n        \"\",\n        \"To retry from this step:\",\n        f\"  superclaude roadmap run {config.spec_file} --resume\",\n        \"\",\n        \"To inspect the failing output:\",\n        f\"  cat {step.output_file}\",\n    ])\n\n    return \"\\n\".join(lines)\n\n\ndef _get_all_step_ids(config: RoadmapConfig) -> list[str]:\n    \"\"\"Get all step IDs in pipeline order.\"\"\"\n    agent_a = config.agents[0]\n    agent_b = config.agents[1] if len(config.agents) > 1 else config.agents[0]\n    return [\n        \"extract\",\n        f\"generate-{agent_a.id}\",\n        f\"generate-{agent_b.id}\",\n        \"diff\",\n        \"debate\",\n        \"score\",\n        \"merge\",\n        \"test-strategy\",\n        \"spec-fidelity\",\n        \"remediate\",\n        \"certify\",\n    ]\n\n\ndef _print_step_start(step: Step) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Print progress output when a step starts.\"\"\"\n    print(f\"[roadmap] Starting step: {step.id}\", flush=True)\n\n\ndef _print_step_complete(step: Step, result: StepResult) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Print progress output when a step completes.\"\"\"\n    duration = f\"{result.duration_seconds:.0f}s\"\n    if result.status == StepStatus.PASS:\n        print(\n            f\"[roadmap] Step {step.id}  PASS (attempt {result.attempt}, {duration})\",\n            flush=True,\n        )\n    else:\n        print(\n            f\"[roadmap] Step {step.id}  {result.status.value} (attempt {result.attempt}, {duration})\",\n            flush=True,\n        )\n        if result.gate_failure_reason:\n            print(f\"           Reason: {result.gate_failure_reason}\", flush=True)\n\n\ndef _dry_run_output(steps: list[Step | list[Step]]) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Print step plan and gate criteria for --dry-run.\"\"\"\n    step_num = 0\n    for entry in steps:\n        if isinstance(entry, list):\n            for s in entry:\n                step_num += 1\n                _print_step_plan(step_num, s, parallel=True)\n        else:\n            step_num += 1\n            _print_step_plan(step_num, entry)\n\n\ndef _print_step_plan(num: int, step: Step, parallel: bool = False) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Print a single step's plan entry.\"\"\"\n    par_label = \" (parallel)\" if parallel else \"\"\n    print(f\"Step {num}{par_label}: {step.id}\")\n    print(f\"  Output: {step.output_file}\")\n    print(f\"  Timeout: {step.timeout_seconds}s\")\n    if step.model:\n        print(f\"  Model: {step.model}\")\n    if step.gate:\n        print(f\"  Gate tier: {step.gate.enforcement_tier}\")\n        print(f\"  Gate min_lines: {step.gate.min_lines}\")\n        if step.gate.required_frontmatter_fields:\n            print(f\"  Gate frontmatter: {', '.join(step.gate.required_frontmatter_fields)}\")\n        if step.gate.semantic_checks:\n            checks = [c.name for c in step.gate.semantic_checks]\n            print(f\"  Semantic checks: {', '.join(checks)}\")\n    print()\n\n\ndef _save_state(\n    config: RoadmapConfig,\n    results: list[StepResult],\n    remediate_metadata: dict | None = None,\n    certify_metadata: dict | None = None,\n) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Write .roadmap-state.json to output_dir via atomic tmp + os.replace().\n\n    Preserves existing ``validation``, ``fidelity_status``, ``remediate``,\n    and ``certify`` keys if present. Accepts optional remediate/certify\n    metadata dicts for Phase 6 state finalization.\n    \"\"\"\n    state_file = config.output_dir / \".roadmap-state.json\"\n    spec_hash = hashlib.sha256(config.spec_file.read_bytes()).hexdigest()\n\n    # Preserve existing keys across state rewrites\n    existing = read_state(state_file)\n    existing_validation = existing.get(\"validation\") if existing else None\n    existing_fidelity = existing.get(\"fidelity_status\") if existing else None\n    existing_remediate = existing.get(\"remediate\") if existing else None\n    existing_certify = existing.get(\"certify\") if existing else None\n\n    state = {\n        \"schema_version\": 1,\n        \"spec_file\": str(config.spec_file),\n        \"spec_hash\": spec_hash,\n        \"agents\": [{\"model\": a.model, \"persona\": a.persona} for a in config.agents],\n        \"depth\": config.depth,\n        \"last_run\": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),\n        \"steps\": {\n            r.step.id: {\n                \"status\": r.status.value,\n                \"attempt\": r.attempt,\n                \"output_file\": str(r.step.output_file),\n                \"started_at\": r.started_at.isoformat(),\n                \"completed_at\": r.finished_at.isoformat(),\n            }\n            for r in results\n            if r.step\n        },\n    }\n\n    if existing_validation is not None:\n        state[\"validation\"] = existing_validation\n\n    # Derive fidelity_status from spec-fidelity step result\n    fidelity_result = next(\n        (r for r in results if r.step and r.step.id == \"spec-fidelity\"),\n        None,\n    )\n    if fidelity_result is not None:\n        state[\"fidelity_status\"] = _derive_fidelity_status(fidelity_result)\n    elif existing_fidelity is not None:\n        state[\"fidelity_status\"] = existing_fidelity\n\n    # Remediate metadata (spec §3.1)\n    if remediate_metadata is not None:\n        state[\"remediate\"] = remediate_metadata\n    elif existing_remediate is not None:\n        state[\"remediate\"] = existing_remediate\n\n    # Certify metadata (spec §3.1)\n    if certify_metadata is not None:\n        state[\"certify\"] = certify_metadata\n    elif existing_certify is not None:\n        state[\"certify\"] = existing_certify\n\n    write_state(state, state_file)\n\n\ndef _derive_fidelity_status(result: StepResult) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Derive fidelity_status enum from a spec-fidelity StepResult.\n\n    Returns one of: 'pass', 'fail', 'skipped', 'degraded'.\n    \"\"\"\n    if result.status == StepStatus.PASS:\n        # Check if the output indicates degraded mode\n        if result.step and result.step.output_file.exists():\n            content = result.step.output_file.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n            if \"validation_complete: false\" in content:\n                return \"degraded\"\n        return \"pass\"\n    if result.status == StepStatus.SKIPPED:\n        return \"skipped\"\n    if result.status in (StepStatus.FAIL, StepStatus.TIMEOUT):\n        return \"fail\"\n    return \"skipped\"\n\n\ndef generate_degraded_report(\n    output_file: Path,\n    failed_agent: str,\n    failure_reason: str,\n) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Generate a degraded fidelity report when the agent fails.\n\n    Produces a report with validation_complete=false and\n    fidelity_check_attempted=true so the SPEC_FIDELITY_GATE\n    can distinguish degraded from clean passes (NFR-007).\n\n    The degraded report names the failed agent and reason in the body.\n    \"\"\"\n    report = (\n        \"---\\n\"\n        \"high_severity_count: 0\\n\"\n        \"medium_severity_count: 0\\n\"\n        \"low_severity_count: 0\\n\"\n        \"total_deviations: 0\\n\"\n        \"validation_complete: false\\n\"\n        \"fidelity_check_attempted: true\\n\"\n        \"tasklist_ready: false\\n\"\n        \"---\\n\"\n        \"\\n\"\n        \"## Degraded Fidelity Report\\n\"\n        \"\\n\"\n        \"Spec-fidelity validation could not be completed.\\n\"\n        \"\\n\"\n        f\"**Failed Agent**: {failed_agent}\\n\"\n        f\"**Failure Reason**: {failure_reason}\\n\"\n        \"\\n\"\n        \"This is a degraded report produced after agent failure and retry \"\n        \"exhaustion. No deviations were analyzed. The pipeline continues \"\n        \"in degraded mode with validation_complete=false.\\n\"\n        \"\\n\"\n        \"### Recommended Actions\\n\"\n        \"\\n\"\n        \"1. Investigate the agent failure\\n\"\n        \"2. Re-run the spec-fidelity step manually\\n\"\n        \"3. Review the roadmap against the specification\\n\"\n    )\n    output_file.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n    output_file.write_text(report, encoding=\"utf-8\")\n\n\ndef build_remediate_metadata(\n    status: str,\n    scope: str,\n    findings_total: int,\n    findings_actionable: int,\n    findings_fixed: int,\n    findings_failed: int,\n    findings_skipped: int,\n    agents_spawned: int,\n    tasklist_file: str,\n) -> dict:\n    \"\"\"Build remediate metadata dict for state schema §3.1.\n\n    Parameters map 1:1 to .roadmap-state.json ``remediate`` entry fields.\n    \"\"\"\n    return {\n        \"status\": status,\n        \"scope\": scope,\n        \"findings_total\": findings_total,\n        \"findings_actionable\": findings_actionable,\n        \"findings_fixed\": findings_fixed,\n        \"findings_failed\": findings_failed,\n        \"findings_skipped\": findings_skipped,\n        \"agents_spawned\": agents_spawned,\n        \"tasklist_file\": tasklist_file,\n        \"timestamp\": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),\n    }\n\n\ndef build_certify_metadata(\n    status: str,\n    findings_verified: int,\n    findings_passed: int,\n    findings_failed: int,\n    certified: bool,\n    report_file: str,\n) -> dict:\n    \"\"\"Build certify metadata dict for state schema §3.1.\n\n    Parameters map 1:1 to .roadmap-state.json ``certify`` entry fields.\n    \"\"\"\n    return {\n        \"status\": status,\n        \"findings_verified\": findings_verified,\n        \"findings_passed\": findings_passed,\n        \"findings_failed\": findings_failed,\n        \"certified\": certified,\n        \"report_file\": report_file,\n        \"timestamp\": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),\n    }\n\n\ndef derive_pipeline_status(state: dict) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Derive the overall pipeline status from state transitions.\n\n    State transitions at each boundary:\n    - post-validate: validated | validated-with-issues\n    - post-remediate: remediated\n    - post-certify: certified | certified-with-caveats\n\n    Returns one of: 'pending', 'validated', 'validated-with-issues',\n    'remediated', 'certified', 'certified-with-caveats'.\n    \"\"\"\n    certify = state.get(\"certify\")\n    if certify is not None:\n        if certify.get(\"certified\", False):\n            return \"certified\"\n        return \"certified-with-caveats\"\n\n    remediate = state.get(\"remediate\")\n    if remediate is not None:\n        return \"remediated\"\n\n    validation = state.get(\"validation\")\n    if validation is not None:\n        if validation.get(\"status\") == \"pass\":\n            return \"validated\"\n        if validation.get(\"status\") == \"fail\":\n            return \"validated-with-issues\"\n\n    return \"pending\"\n\n\ndef write_state(state: dict, path: Path) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Write state dict to path atomically via tmp file + os.replace().\"\"\"\n    import os as _os\n\n    path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n    tmp = path.with_suffix(\".tmp\")\n    tmp.write_text(json.dumps(state, indent=2), encoding=\"utf-8\")\n    _os.replace(str(tmp), str(path))\n\n\ndef read_state(path: Path) -> dict | None:\n    \"\"\"Read state from path with graceful recovery on missing/malformed files.\"\"\"\n    if not path.exists():\n        return None\n    try:\n        text = path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n        if not text.strip():\n            return None\n        return json.loads(text)\n    except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):\n        return None\n\n\ndef apply_decomposition_pass(deliverables: list[Deliverable]) -> list[Deliverable]:\n    \"\"\"Post-generation decomposition pass for the roadmap pipeline.\n\n    Runs after deliverable generation, before output formatting.\n    Splits behavioral deliverables into Implement/Verify pairs.\n\n    Idempotent: running twice produces identical results because\n    already-decomposed deliverables (IDs ending .a/.b) are skipped.\n\n    Preserves milestone-internal ordering: deliverables within each\n    milestone maintain their relative order after decomposition.\n    \"\"\"\n    return decompose_deliverables(deliverables)\n\n\ndef execute_roadmap(\n    config: RoadmapConfig,\n    resume: bool = False,\n    no_validate: bool = False,\n    auto_accept: bool = False,\n) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Execute the roadmap generation pipeline.\n\n    Builds the step list, handles --dry-run and --resume, then\n    delegates to execute_pipeline() with roadmap_run_step.\n\n    After all 9 steps pass, auto-invokes validation unless\n    --no-validate is set or --resume halted on a failed step.\n\n    Note: --no-validate does NOT skip the spec-fidelity step\n    (FR-010, AC-005). It only skips the post-pipeline validation\n    subsystem.\n\n    Args:\n        auto_accept: When True, the spec-patch resume cycle skips the\n            interactive prompt and proceeds automatically if evidence is\n            found. When False (default), the cycle prompts the user.\n            This is an internal parameter, not exposed on the CLI.\n    \"\"\"\n    config.output_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n\n    # FR-2.24.1.11: Recursion guard — local variable, per-invocation\n    _spec_patch_cycle_count = 0\n\n    # FR-2.24.1.9 Condition 3: Capture spec hash at function entry\n    initial_spec_hash = hashlib.sha256(config.spec_file.read_bytes()).hexdigest()\n\n    steps = _build_steps(config)\n\n    # --dry-run: print plan and exit\n    if config.dry_run:\n        _dry_run_output(steps)\n        return\n\n    # --resume: check which steps already pass their gates\n    if resume:\n        from ..pipeline.gates import gate_passed\n\n        steps = _apply_resume(steps, config, gate_passed)\n\n    # Execute pipeline\n    results = execute_pipeline(\n        steps=steps,\n        config=config,\n        run_step=roadmap_run_step,\n        on_step_start=_print_step_start,\n        on_step_complete=_print_step_complete,\n    )\n\n    # Save state\n    _save_state(config, results)\n\n    # Check for failures\n    failures = [r for r in results if r.status in (StepStatus.FAIL, StepStatus.TIMEOUT)]\n    if failures:\n        # FR-2.24.1.9: Check if spec-fidelity failed and auto-resume is possible\n        spec_fidelity_failed = any(\n            r.step and r.step.id == \"spec-fidelity\"\n            and r.status in (StepStatus.FAIL, StepStatus.TIMEOUT)\n            for r in results\n        )\n\n        if spec_fidelity_failed:\n            resumed = _apply_resume_after_spec_patch(\n                config=config,\n                results=results,\n                auto_accept=auto_accept,\n                initial_spec_hash=initial_spec_hash,\n                cycle_count=_spec_patch_cycle_count,\n            )\n            if resumed:\n                _spec_patch_cycle_count += 1\n                # Cycle complete — resumed pipeline ran to completion or failure\n                # inside _apply_resume_after_spec_patch. If it returned True,\n                # we're done (either success or failure was handled internally).\n                return\n\n        halt_msg = _format_halt_output(results, config)\n        print(halt_msg, file=sys.stderr)\n        sys.exit(1)\n\n    print(f\"\\n[roadmap] Pipeline complete: {len(results)} steps passed\", flush=True)\n\n    # Auto-invoke validation after successful pipeline completion\n    if no_validate:\n        print(\"[roadmap] Validation skipped (--no-validate)\", flush=True)\n        _save_validation_status(config, \"skipped\")\n        return\n\n    # Check if validation already completed (--resume path)\n    if resume:\n        state_file = config.output_dir / \".roadmap-state.json\"\n        state = read_state(state_file)\n        if state and \"validation\" in state:\n            saved = state[\"validation\"]\n            if saved.get(\"status\") in (\"pass\", \"fail\"):\n                print(\n                    f\"[roadmap] Validation already completed ({saved['status']}), skipping\",\n                    flush=True,\n                )\n                return\n\n    _auto_invoke_validate(config)\n\n\n\ndef _find_qualifying_deviation_files(\n    config: RoadmapConfig,\n    results: list[StepResult],\n) -> list:\n    \"\"\"Find deviation files written after spec-fidelity started.\n\n    Returns qualifying DeviationRecord objects, or empty list if\n    conditions are not met.\n\n    Implementation detail — not specified in the spec. Extracted for\n    testability of the three-condition detection gate.\n    \"\"\"\n    from .spec_patch import scan_accepted_deviation_records\n\n    state_file = config.output_dir / \".roadmap-state.json\"\n    state = read_state(state_file)\n    if state is None:\n        return []\n\n    # Get spec-fidelity started_at timestamp\n    steps_state = state.get(\"steps\", {})\n    fidelity_state = steps_state.get(\"spec-fidelity\", {})\n    started_at_str = fidelity_state.get(\"started_at\")\n\n    # Fail-closed: if started_at is absent, condition not met\n    if not started_at_str:\n        return []\n\n    try:\n        started_at_ts = datetime.fromisoformat(started_at_str).timestamp()\n    except (ValueError, TypeError):\n        return []\n\n    # Scan all deviation records\n    all_records = scan_accepted_deviation_records(config.output_dir)\n    if not all_records:\n        return []\n\n    # Filter to records written AFTER spec-fidelity started.\n    # Strict > (not >=): a record with mtime == started_at was written before\n    # or exactly at step start, so it cannot be evidence of a subprocess fix.\n    # Caveat: HFS+ and some NFS mounts have 1-second mtime resolution, so\n    # records created within the same second as started_at may be missed.\n    qualifying = [r for r in all_records if r.mtime > started_at_ts]\n    return qualifying\n\n\ndef _apply_resume_after_spec_patch(\n    config: RoadmapConfig,\n    results: list[StepResult],\n    auto_accept: bool,\n    initial_spec_hash: str,\n    cycle_count: int,\n) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Attempt a single spec-patch auto-resume cycle after spec-fidelity FAIL.\n\n    Evaluates the three-condition detection gate (FR-2.24.1.9):\n      1. Recursion guard: cycle_count == 0\n      2. Qualifying deviation files exist with mtime > started_at\n      3. Spec file hash changed since run started (initial_spec_hash)\n\n    If all conditions pass, executes the six-step disk-reread sequence\n    (FR-2.24.1.10) and re-runs the pipeline via _apply_resume.\n\n    Single-writer assumption: no concurrent writer modifies\n    .roadmap-state.json between the reread and write steps.\n\n    Returns True if the cycle was attempted (regardless of outcome),\n    False if conditions were not met.\n    \"\"\"\n    # FR-2.24.1.11: Recursion guard\n    if cycle_count >= 1:\n        print(\n            \"[roadmap] Spec-patch cycle already exhausted \"\n            f\"(cycle_count={cycle_count}). Proceeding to normal failure.\",\n            flush=True,\n        )\n        return False\n\n    # FR-2.24.1.9 Condition 2: Qualifying deviation files\n    qualifying = _find_qualifying_deviation_files(config, results)\n    if not qualifying:\n        return False\n\n    # FR-2.24.1.9 Condition 3: Spec hash changed since run started\n    current_hash = hashlib.sha256(config.spec_file.read_bytes()).hexdigest()\n    if current_hash == initial_spec_hash:\n        return False\n\n    # All three conditions met — enter the cycle\n    # FR-2.24.1.12: Cycle entry logging\n    print(\n        f\"[roadmap] Spec patched by subprocess. \"\n        f\"Found {len(qualifying)} accepted deviation record(s).\",\n        flush=True,\n    )\n    print(\n        \"[roadmap] Triggering spec-hash sync and resume (cycle 1/1).\",\n        flush=True,\n    )\n\n    # FR-2.24.1.10: Six-step disk-reread sequence\n    state_file = config.output_dir / \".roadmap-state.json\"\n\n    # Step 1: Re-read state from disk\n    _fresh_state = read_state(state_file)  # noqa: F841\n\n    # Step 2: Recompute spec hash\n    new_hash = hashlib.sha256(config.spec_file.read_bytes()).hexdigest()\n\n    # Step 3: Atomic write of new hash\n    try:\n        # Read again for fresh copy, update only spec_hash, write atomically\n        fresh_for_write = read_state(state_file) or {}\n        fresh_for_write[\"spec_hash\"] = new_hash\n        write_state(fresh_for_write, state_file)\n    except OSError as exc:\n        print(\n            f\"[roadmap] ERROR: Failed to update spec_hash during \"\n            f\"auto-resume cycle: {exc}. Falling through to normal failure.\",\n            file=sys.stderr,\n            flush=True,\n        )\n        return True  # Cycle was attempted but failed — don't retry\n\n    # Step 4: Re-read state from disk AGAIN (this is what _apply_resume gets)\n    post_write_state = read_state(state_file)\n    if post_write_state is None:\n        print(\n            \"[roadmap] ERROR: Could not re-read state after write. \"\n            \"Falling through to normal failure.\",\n            file=sys.stderr,\n            flush=True,\n        )\n        return True\n\n    # Step 5: Rebuild steps\n    steps = _build_steps(config)\n\n    # Step 6: Apply resume with post-write state\n    from ..pipeline.gates import gate_passed\n\n    steps = _apply_resume(steps, config, gate_passed)\n\n    # Re-execute pipeline from the resumed point\n    resumed_results = execute_pipeline(\n        steps=steps,\n        config=config,\n        run_step=roadmap_run_step,\n        on_step_start=_print_step_start,\n        on_step_complete=_print_step_complete,\n    )\n\n    # Save state from resumed run\n    _save_state(config, resumed_results)\n\n    # FR-2.24.1.12: Cycle completion logging\n    print(\"[roadmap] Spec-patch resume cycle complete.\", flush=True)\n\n    # Check if resumed pipeline also failed\n    resumed_failures = [\n        r for r in resumed_results\n        if r.status in (StepStatus.FAIL, StepStatus.TIMEOUT)\n    ]\n    if resumed_failures:\n        # FR-2.24.1.13: Normal failure on cycle exhaustion\n        # Use second-run results only\n        halt_msg = _format_halt_output(resumed_results, config)\n        print(halt_msg, file=sys.stderr)\n        sys.exit(1)\n\n    # Resumed pipeline succeeded\n    print(\n        f\"\\n[roadmap] Pipeline complete: {len(resumed_results)} steps passed\",\n        flush=True,\n    )\n    return True\n\ndef _save_validation_status(\n    config: RoadmapConfig,\n    status: str,\n) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Update .roadmap-state.json with validation status.\n\n    Adds or updates the ``validation`` key without modifying existing state.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    config:\n        RoadmapConfig with output_dir.\n    status:\n        One of \"pass\", \"fail\", or \"skipped\".\n    \"\"\"\n    state_file = config.output_dir / \".roadmap-state.json\"\n    state = read_state(state_file)\n    if state is None:\n        state = {}\n    state[\"validation\"] = {\n        \"status\": status,\n        \"timestamp\": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),\n    }\n    write_state(state, state_file)\n\n\ndef _auto_invoke_validate(config: RoadmapConfig) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Auto-invoke validation after a successful roadmap pipeline run.\n\n    Inherits --model, --max-turns, --debug from the parent roadmap config.\n    Default agent count for roadmap-run invocation is 2 (dual-agent for rigor).\n    \"\"\"\n    from .models import AgentSpec, ValidateConfig\n    from .validate_executor import execute_validate\n\n    # Passes REMEDIATE_GATE\n    3. source_report_hash matches current validation report SHA-256\n\n    Returns False (re-run needed) otherwise.\n    \"\"\"\n    from .gates import REMEDIATE_GATE\n\n    tasklist_file = config.output_dir / \"remediation-tasklist.md\"\n    if not tasklist_file.exists():\n        return False\n\n    passed, _reason = gate_fn(tasklist_file, REMEDIATE_GATE)\n    if not passed:\n        return False\n\n    # Hash check: verify tasklist was generated from current validation report\n    if not _check_tasklist_hash_current(tasklist_file, config.output_dir):\n        print(\n            \"[roadmap] Remediation tasklist stale (hash mismatch), will re-run remediate\",\n            flush=True,\n        )\n        return False\n\n    return True\n\n\ndef check_certify_resume(\n    config: RoadmapConfig,\n    gate_fn: Callable,\n) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Check if the certify step can be skipped on --resume.\n\n    Returns True (skip) when:\n    1. certification-report.md exists\n    2. Passes CERTIFY_GATE\n\n    Returns False (re-run needed) otherwise.\n    \"\"\"\n    from .gates import CERTIFY_GATE\n\n    report_file = config.output_dir / \"certification-report.md\"\n    if not report_file.exists():\n        return False\n\n    passed, _reason = gate_fn(report_file, CERTIFY_GATE)\n    return passed\n\n\ndef _check_tasklist_hash_current(\n    tasklist_file: Path,\n    output_dir: Path,\n) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Check if remediation tasklist's source_report_hash matches current report.\n\n    Reads the YAML frontmatter source_report_hash and compares against\n    SHA-256 of the validation report file. Returns False on mismatch\n    (fail closed).\n    \"\"\"\n    from .gates import _parse_frontmatter\n\n    content = tasklist_file.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n    fm = _parse_frontmatter(content)\n    if fm is None:\n        return False\n\n    saved_hash = fm.get(\"source_report_hash\", \"\")\n    if not saved_hash:\n        return False\n\n    # Default to reflect-merged.md or merged-validation-report.md\n        report_path = output_dir / \"reflect-merged.md\"\n        if not report_path.exists():\n            report_path = output_dir / \"merged-validation-report.md\"\n\n    if not report_path.exists():\n        return False\n\n    current_hash = hashlib.sha256(report_path.read_bytes()).hexdigest()\n    return saved_hash == current_hash\n\n\ndef _apply_resume(\n    steps: list[Step | list[Step]],\n    config: RoadmapConfig,\n    gate_fn: Callable,\n) -> list[Step | list[Step]]:\n    \"\"\"Apply --resume logic: skip steps whose outputs already pass gates.\n\n    Also checks for stale spec detection.\n    \"\"\"\n    state_file = config.output_dir / \".roadmap-state.json\"\n    state = read_state(state_file)\n    force_extract = False\n    if state is not None:\n        saved_hash = state.get(\"spec_hash\", \"\")\n        current_hash = hashlib.sha256(config.spec_file.read_bytes()).hexdigest()\n        if saved_hash and saved_hash != current_hash:\n            print(\n                f\"WARNING: spec-file has changed since last run.\\n\"\n                f\"  Last hash: {saved_hash[:12]}...\\n\"\n                f\"  Current:   {current_hash[:12]}...\\n\"\n                f\"Forcing re-run of extract step.\",\n                file=sys.stderr,\n                flush=True,\n            )\n            force_extract = True\n\n    skipped = 0\n    result: list[Step | list[Step]] = []\n    found_failure = False\n\n    for entry in steps:\n        if found_failure:\n            # After first failing step, include all remaining steps\n            result.append(entry)\n            continue\n\n        if isinstance(entry, list):\n            # Parallel group: check all steps\n            all_pass = True\n            for s in entry:\n                if s.gate:\n                    passed, _reason = gate_fn(s.output_file, s.gate)\n                    if not passed:\n                        all_pass = False\n                        break\n                else:\n                    all_pass = False\n                    break\n            if all_pass:\n                skipped += len(entry)\n                print(f\"[roadmap] Skipping {', '.join(s.id for s in entry)} (gates pass)\", flush=True)\n            else:\n                found_failure = True\n                result.append(entry)\n        else:\n            # Force re-run of extract on stale spec\n            if force_extract and entry.id == \"extract\":\n                found_failure = True\n                result.append(entry)\n                continue\n\n            if entry.gate:\n                passed, _reason = gate_fn(entry.output_file, entry.gate)\n                if passed:\n                    skipped += 1\n                    print(f\"[roadmap] Skipping {entry.id} (gate passes)\", flush=True)\n                    continue\n            found_failure = True\n            result.append(entry)\n\n    if skipped > 0:\n        print(f\"[roadmap] Skipped {skipped} steps (gates pass)\", flush=True)\n\n    if not result:\n        print(\"[roadmap] All steps already pass gates. Nothing to do.\", flush=True)\n\n    return result\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":518,"oldLines":6,"newStart":518,"newLines":211,"lines":["     ]"," "," ","+def _print_terminal_halt(","+    output_dir: Path,","+    remaining_findings: list,","+    attempt_count: int,","+    spec_patch_budget_exhausted: bool = False,","+    file=None,","+) -> None:","+    \"\"\"Print terminal halt message to stderr with finding details (SC-6).","+","+    Outputs:","+    - Attempt count and remaining failing finding count","+    - Per-finding details (ID, severity, description)","+    - Manual-fix instructions including certification report path and resume command","+","+    Args:","+        output_dir: Pipeline output directory (for certification report path)","+        remaining_findings: List of Finding objects that failed remediation","+        attempt_count: Number of remediation attempts made","+        spec_patch_budget_exhausted: If True, note that spec-patch budget is also exhausted","+        file: Output stream (defaults to sys.stderr)","+    \"\"\"","+    if file is None:","+        file = sys.stderr","+","+    certify_path = output_dir / \"certify.md\"","+","+    lines = [","+        \"\",","+        \"=\" * 72,","+        \"TERMINAL HALT: Remediation budget exhausted\",","+        \"=\" * 72,","+        f\"Attempt count: {attempt_count}\",","+        f\"Remaining failing findings: {len(remaining_findings)}\",","+        \"\",","+    ]","+","+    if remaining_findings:","+        lines.append(\"Failing findings:\")","+        for finding in remaining_findings:","+            severity = getattr(finding, \"severity\", \"UNKNOWN\")","+            description = getattr(finding, \"description\", \"\")","+            finding_id = getattr(finding, \"id\", \"UNKNOWN\")","+            lines.append(f\"  - {finding_id} [{severity}]: {description}\")","+        lines.append(\"\")","+","+    lines.extend([","+        \"Manual fix required:\",","+        f\"  1. Real wall-clock measurement requires live pipeline runs.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def test_overhead_calculation_within_budget(self):\n        \"\"\"Overhead formula: (steps_10_11_time / steps_1_9_time) * 100 <= 30%.\"\"\"\n        # Simulate: steps 1-9 took 100s, steps 10-11 took 25s\n        steps_1_9_time = 100.0\n        steps_10_11_time = 25.0\n        overhead = (steps_10_11_time / steps_1_9_time) * 100\n        assert overhead <= 30.0, f\"Overhead {overhead:.1f}% exceeds 30% budget\"\n\n    def test_overhead_calculation_at_boundary(self):\n        \"\"\"Exactly 30% overhead is within budget.\"\"\"\n        steps_1_9_time = 100.0\n        steps_10_11_time = 30.0\n        overhead = (steps_10_11_time / steps_1_9_time) * 100\n        assert overhead <= 30.0\n\n    def test_overhead_calculation_exceeds_budget(self):\n        \"\"\"Over 30% overhead would fail the budget check.\"\"\"\n        steps_1_9_time = 100.0\n        steps_10_11_time = 31.0\n        overhead = each.\"\"\"\n        findings = [_make_finding(f\"F-{i:02d}\") for i in range(20)]\n        source = \"# Report\\n\" * 100\n\n        # Measure tasklist generation\n        start = generation took {tasklist_time:.3f}s\"\n\n        # Measure certification report generation\n        results = [\n            {\"finding_id\": f.id, \"result\": \"PASS\", \"justification\": \"Fixed\"}\n            for f in findings\n        ]\n        start = time.perf_counter()\n        generate_certification_report(results, findings)\n        cert_time = time.perf_counter() - start\n        assert cert_time < 0.1, f\"Certification generation took {cert_time:.3f}s\"\n\n    def test_state_timing_extraction(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"Timing data can be extracted from .roadmap-state.json timestamps.\"\"\"\n        from superclaude.cli.roadmap.executor import write_state, read_state\n\n        state = {\n            \"steps\": {\n                \"extract\": {\n                    \"started_at\": \"2026-03-10T00:00:00+00:00\",\n                    \"completed_at\": \"2026-03-10T00:01:00+00:00\",\n                },\n                \"spec-fidelity\": {\n                    \"started_at\": \"2026-03-10T00:09:00+00:00\",\n                    \"completed_at\": \"2026-03-10T00:10:00+00:00\",\n                },\n            },\n            \"remediate\": {\n                \"timestamp\": \"2026-03-10T00:10:30+00:00\",\n            },\n            \"certify\": {\n                \"timestamp\": \"2026-03-10T00:11:00+00:00\",\n            },\n        }\n        state_file = tmp_path / \".roadmap-state.json\"\n        write_state(state, state_file)\n\n        loaded = read_state(state_file)\n        assert loaded is not None\n\n        # Extract timing from state\n        extract_start = datetime.fromisoformat(loaded[\"steps\"][\"extract\"][\"started_at\"])\n        fidelity_end = datetime.fromisoformat(loaded[\"steps\"][\"spec-fidelity\"][\"completed_at\"])\n        steps_1_9 = (fidelity_end - extract_start).total_seconds()\n\n        certify_ts = datetime.fromisoformat(loaded[\"certify\"][\"timestamp\"])\n        fidelity_ts = datetime.fromisoformat(loaded[\"steps\"][\"spec-fidelity\"][\"completed_at\"])\n        steps_10_11 = (certify_ts - fidelity_ts).total_seconds()\n\n        overhead = (steps_10_11 / steps_1_9) * 100\n        assert overhead <= 30.0, f\"Overhead {overhead:.1f}% exceeds 30%\"\n\n\n# \"\"\"Edge cases: SIGINT recovery, out-of-allowlist, zero-findings, fallback parser.\"\"\"\n\n    # --- Edge Case 1: SIGINT leaves .pre-remediate files ---\n\n    def test_sigint_leaves_snapshots_for_recovery(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"SIGINT during remediation: .pre-remediate files remain for manual recovery.\"\"\"\n        target = tmp_path / \"roadmap.md\"\n        target.write_text(\"# Original content\\n\")\n\n        # Create snapshots\n        snapshot_paths = create_snapshots([str(target)])\n        assert len(snapshot_paths) == remediation\n        target.write_text(\"# Modified by agent\\n\")\n\n        # Simulate SIGINT: don't call cleanup_snapshots\n        # Verify snapshot still exists for manual recovery\n        assert snapshot.exists()\n        assert snapshot.read_text() == \"# Original content\\n\"\n\n        # Manual recovery possible via restore\n        restore_from_snapshots([str(target)])\n        assert target.read_text() == \"# Original content\\n\"\n\n    def test_snapshot_restore_atomicity(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"Restore uses os.replace() for atomicity.\"\"\"\n        target = Document\\n\"\n            \"## 1.0 Introduction\\n\"\n            \"See section 9.9 for details.\\n\"\n        )\n        import warnings\n        with warnings.catch_warnings(record=True) as w:\n \"1:3\",\n    # SC-2: D-02 (DEV-004 INTENTIONAL) and D-04 (DEV-003 PRE_APPROVED) pre-approved\n    # high_severity_count=0 because INTENTIONAL and PRE_APPROVED excluded\n    \"high_severity_count\": \"0\",\n    \"medium_severity_count\": \"0\",\n    \"low_severity_count\": \"0\",\n    \"total_deviations\": \"4\",\n    \"validation_complete\": \"true\",\n    \"tasklist_ready\": \"true\",\n    # Deviation analysis: SC-3 DEV-001 and DEV-002 are SLIP (routed to fix_roadmap)\n    \"schema_version\": \"1.0\",\n    \"total_analyzed\": \"4\",\n    \"slip_count\": \"2\",\n    \"intentional_count\": \"1\",\n    \"pre_approved_count\": \"1\",\n    \"ambiguous_count\": \"0\",\n    \"ambiguous_deviations\": \"0\",\n    \"routing_fix_roadmap\": \"DEV-001 DEV-002\",\n    \"routing_update_spec\": \"\",\n    \"routing_no_action\": \"DEV-003 DEV-004\",\n    \"routing_human_review\": \"\",\n    \"analysis_complete\": \"true\",\n    # Remediation\n    \"type\": \"remediation-tasklist\",\n    \"source_report\": \"spec-deviations.md\",\n    \"source_report_hash\": \"abc123\",\n    \"total_findings\": \"2\",\n    \"actionable\": \"2\",\n    \"skipped\": \"2\",\n    # Certification: SC-1 pipeline completes with certified=true\n    \"findings_verified\": \"2\",\n    \"findings_passed\": \"2\",\n    \"findings_failed\": \"0\",\n    \"certified\": \"true\",\n    \"certification_date\": \"2026-03-10\",\n}\n\n\ndef _build_step_content(step: Step) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Build gate-passing content for a step using v2.24 fixture values.\"\"\"\n    if step.gate is None:\n        return \"## Step output\\n- Done\\n\"\n\n    fm_fields: dict[str, str] = {}\n    for f in step.gate.required_frontmatter_fields:\n        fm_fields[f] = _V224_FM_VALUES.get(f, \"test_value\")\n\n    lines = [\"---\"]\n    for k, v in fm_fields.items():\n        lines.append(f\"{k}: {v}\")\n    lines.append(\"---\")\n    lines.append(\"## Output\")\n\n    # Add enough content lines to satisfy min_lines\n    min_needed = step.gate.min_lines if step.gate else 10\n    for i in range(max(min_needed, 20)):\n        lines.append(f\"- Item {i}: content for step {step.id}\")\n\n    # Add certification table for certify step\n    if step.id == \"certify\":\n        lines.extend([\n            \"\",\n            \"## Certification Results\",\n            \"\",\n            \"| Finding | Severity | Result | Justification |\",\n            \"| --- | --- | --- | --- |\",\n            \"| F-001 | BLOCKING | PASSED | Added Success Criteria section |\",\n            \"| F-002 | BLOCKING | PASSED | Added Performance Budget table |\",\n            \"\",\n        ])\n\n    return \"\\n\".join(lines)\n\n\ndef _mock_runner_all_pass(step: Step, cfg: RoadmapConfig, cancel_check):\n    \"\"\"Mock runner that produces gate-passing output for every step.\"\"\"\n    content = _build_step_content(step)\n    step.output_file.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n    step.output_file.write_text(content, encoding=\"utf-8\")\n    return StepResult(\n        step=step,\n        status=StepStatus.PASS,\n        attempt=1,\n        started_at=_now(),\n        finished_at=_now(),\n    )\n\n\n# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n# SC-1: Pipeline completes to certify without manual intervention\n# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n\nclass TestSC1PipelineComplete:\n    \"\"\"SC-1: Full pipeline run completes without halting at any step.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_pipeline_reaches_certify(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"All 9 steps pass; pipeline completes to certify step.\"\"\"\n        config = _make_config(tmp_path)\n        steps = == 9\n        assert all(r.status == StepStatus.PASS for r in results)\n\n    def test_certify_step_is_last(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"certify is the final step in the pipeline (no halts before 0\n\n\n# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n# SC-2: Pre-approved deviations excluded from HIGH count\n#   def test_v224_budget_enforcement_sc6_semantics(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"SC-6: Third remediation attempt on v2.24 scenario triggers terminal halt.\"\"\"\n        # Write state showing 2
